reviews.review_msg: Lectures are asynchronous and given in a podcast format every week. Weekly short stories aren't too long and weekly podcast lectures are not long either. Lectures are amazing in the way the prof explains everything going on in the story with detail and good reasoning. The reading journals and final essay aren't too bad as long as you pay attention.

Dr. Colligan provided very insightful lectures revolving around The New Yorker podcasts. The class was very straightforward, not course work heavy, having only a few journals and essays to complete. The tutorials were led by separate TAs and work was graded by them instead of Dr. Colligan. Feedback was dependent on the TA.

I thought the prof was great! She explained the stories we had to read in her weekly podcasts and I learned a lot from them. For a first year course, I thought the grading was pretty hard though. There was 2 journal entries and 2 essays and the grading is very subjective as it's based on how your TA perceives it.

Extremely disappointed in the marking done in this class. After my first journal entries, I decided to get help from the Prof to improve my next entry as well as improve my essay. I ended up getting significantly lower marks. I wouldnt recommend that anyone take an English course just because of how subjective the marking in the class can be.

Theres no way to get a good grade in this class. All the journals and essay markings are subjective and depend on what the TAs think is good. Theres no concrete way of getting a good mark and there is little effort put into helping the students improve their essays. Instead, we are provided with vague comments on why we didnt get full marks,

For online, you will likely not interact with Prof. Colligan. Most of your interaction will be with your TA, so experiences may vary. That being said, New Yorker stories is full of short stories you will enjoy. This is likely the first english class I have enjoyed the readings in. Expect 1-2 hours of reading a week, and 2-3 essays for the course.

Colette Colligan runs a completely asynchronous podcast-style course which makes it convenient to listen to her lectures while completing other tasks. Her reading list is engaging and varied. She only assigns 1-2 short stories per week, ranging from 1-25 pages so far. There are very few written assignments for a "W" course. I absolutely recommend!

I like her reading list. Dr Colligan's assignments were exciting to do, but they needed to be completed by software which was sometimes tricky. But the technology increased my interests to do homework and explore knowledge. The lectures were relaxed. I really enjoyed taking Dr Colligan's class.

Dr. Colligan is very passionate about her class. She makes the classroom environment very friendly and open to questions. She responds to emails quickly and gives fantastic feedback. I would definitely take another class with her.

English is my least favourite subject but Colette made this an enjoyable class.  The lectures were informative in that it helped facilitate discussion in tutorial, and helped with the blog/essay writing, and probably writing the final too.  Lectures went at a good pace, but can be a bit dry at times due to the content itself.
